title: service-accounts — tenancy hooks
description: service-accounts has NO tenancy hooks. The module owns a table (`service_accounts`) that CAN carry `tenant_id`, but no `TenantProvisioned` action mutates the table — SAs are provisioned by a platform_admin operator via HTTP or CLI, never automatically as a side-effect of tenant creation.
rationale: Tenancy hooks are for state that must materialise on tenant provisioning (default rows, seed data, per-tenant tables). Service accounts are provisioned intentionally by operators — nothing is auto-created when a tenant lands.

Similarly, TenantErased does not cascade to `service_accounts` because the FK `tenant_id` uses CASCADE at the DB layer — SAs scoped to a deleted tenant are hard-deleted by DB constraint, no application-layer hook needed. Platform-plane SAs (`tenant_id IS NULL`) are untouched by tenant lifecycle events.

Explicitly declaring `hooks: []` is a design invariant — any PR that adds a tenancy hook here is mis-scoping the module. If an ops workflow genuinely needs auto-provisioning of SAs on tenant creation, ship it as a separate `platform/onboarding` module concern that ORCHESTRATES the SA provisioner, not as a hook on this substrate.
